adjudication.disposition: Round 1's four findings were addressed in a first repair pass. A second B dispatch (round 2) found that the code-block caption finding had NOT actually been resolved (the fragment 'Command shape:' had only been prefixed with 'The', not given the explanatory clause siblings carry) and that the round-1 repair for the jargon finding had itself introduced a new sentence-fragment defect ('refuses with,' with no object). Per ADR-0017's two-round cap, rather than spawning a third B round the orchestrator applied both repairs directly: the command-shape caption now names what the shown --core-schema/--research-schema flags do before the code block; the record_reading.py Related bullet's dangling 'refuses with,' now reads 'refusing with a stated reason, rather than allowing a silent bad write.'

Beyond ADR-0017's 2-round cap -- which this gate's own schema enforces structurally via MAX_ROUNDS=2, so a genuine 3rd round cannot be represented as a third 'rounds' entry -- one further confirming fresh-context pass was run on the round-2-repaired document (agentId aa856e5f5ed77552f). It found 5 more findings, all mechanical legibility polish rather than substantive disagreement: an unglossed document-wide 'ledger' vs 'record' terminology split, one unexplained HMAC acronym, one link-text section title truncated relative to its target heading, one forward reference to 'stamp' used before its §5 definition, and 4 sibling §7 bullets missing verbs. All 5 were repaired; this record's content_sha256 is computed from the document's state after every one of the three rounds' repairs, so no known finding from any round is outstanding in the content this attestation covers. No 4th round was run, to avoid open-ended grinding past an already-exceeded cap. This record is marked escalated (rather than a clean 2-round convergence) precisely because the process ran one round beyond ADR-0017's literal limit -- the honest fact being recorded here, following the precedent in BACKLOG.md's 'First live enforcement of ADR-0017's loop' entry (2026-07-11), which recorded an analogous 3-invocation deviation the same way rather than either silently grinding or silently under-reporting.

adjudication.adjudicated_by: accounting-stage-a builder (self-adjudicated, orchestrator-ratified pattern per the tracker item's own brief)
adjudication.disposition: Declined to repair any of round 1's 8 findings, and declined a redundant round-2 re-review of unchanged content. All 8 findings sit in GLOSSARY.md entries this tracker item did not touch (### autoharn, ### Pillar / Pillar 1/2/3, ### extreme auditability / deductive maintenance, ### world, ### obligation, ### scratch schema, ### stamp, ### decomposition-review-blocker) -- pre-existing legibility debt, most of it a systemic fragment-lead pattern repeated across the majority of the file's entries, not a defect introduced by this edit. The two entries this tracker item actually added/changed (### mandated (tier), ### forbidden (tier)) drew zero findings from B. ADR-0017 Rule 4 states an edit to an existing document obligates 'at minimum the edited sections and the document's opening' to standard, not the whole back-catalog, and its own 'What this tenet does NOT mean' section names 'a roving legibility sweep' wearing the tenet's clothes as an ADR-0004 violation, not compliance. Escalated here (after one round, not the usual two) because no in-scope repair exists to attempt -- filing this as a pre-existing-debt disclosure for the orchestrator/seam to route to a maintainer-initiated GLOSSARY.md sweep if wanted, rather than running a second B pass over content deliberately left unchanged.
